#deck:GitHub Copilot
Front
column2: Back

column1:
What AI system powers GitHub Copilot?

  • A) GPT-3
  • B) OpenAI Codex
  • C) BERT
  • D) LaMDA
column2:
✓ B) OpenAI Codex

GitHub Copilot is powered by the OpenAI Codex system, which was trained on a dataset that included a larger concentration of public source code than GPT-3.

column1:
Which GitHub Copilot plan includes 2,000 code completions and 50 chat requests per month?

  • A) Copilot Pro
  • B) Copilot Business
  • C) Copilot Free
  • D) Copilot Enterprise
column2:
✓ C) Copilot Free

GitHub Copilot Free includes 2,000 code completions per month, 50 chat requests per month, and access to both GPT-4o and Claude 3.5 Sonnet models.

column1:
Which Copilot subscription plan is designed for large organizations and includes personalized code suggestions based on internal/private code?

  • A) Copilot Pro+
  • B) Copilot Business
  • C) Copilot Enterprise
  • D) Copilot Pro
column2:
✓ C) Copilot Enterprise

GitHub Copilot Enterprise is designed for large organizations, offering personalized code suggestions based on internal/private code, integration with GitHub Enterprise Cloud, and organization-wide customization.

column1:
What are Copilot's inline code suggestions called?

  • A) Phantom text
  • B) Ghost text
  • C) Shadow code
  • D) Hint text
column2:
✓ B) Ghost text

When GitHub Copilot is enabled, it provides suggestions called ghost text. You can ignore it or accept it by pressing the Tab key.

column1:
Which keyboard shortcut opens inline chat in VS Code on a Mac?

  • A) Ctrl+Shift+I
  • B) Cmd+Shift+C
  • C) Command+I
  • D) Cmd+Alt+C
column2:
✓ C) Command+I

On a Mac, you can access inline chat by using Command+I. On Windows, the shortcut is Ctrl+I.

column1:
Which slash command generates unit tests for selected code?

  • A) /doc
  • B) /generate
  • C) /optimize
  • D) /tests
column2:
✓ D) /tests

The /tests slash command creates unit tests for the selected code. Other commands include /doc (add comments), /explain (get explanations), and /optimize (improve runtime).

column1:
Which slash command adds comments/documentation to selected code?

  • A) /comment
  • B) /doc
  • C) /explain
  • D) /annotate
column2:
✓ B) /doc

The /doc slash command adds comments to the specified or selected code. The /explain command gets explanations about the code.

column1:
How many principles make up Microsoft and GitHub's Responsible AI framework?

  • A) Four
  • B) Five
  • C) Six
  • D) Seven
column2:
✓ C) Six

Microsoft and GitHub's Responsible AI framework consists of six principles: Fairness, Reliability and Safety, Privacy and Security, Inclusiveness, Transparency, and Accountability.

column1:
Which Responsible AI principle states that AI systems should treat all people fairly?

  • A) Inclusiveness
  • B) Transparency
  • C) Fairness
  • D) Accountability
column2:
✓ C) Fairness

The Fairness principle states that AI systems should treat all people fairly. Techniques include reviewing training data, testing models with balanced demographic samples, and using adversarial debiasing.

column1:
Which Responsible AI principle states that people should be responsible for how AI systems operate?

  • A) Transparency
  • B) Reliability and Safety
  • C) Inclusiveness
  • D) Accountability
column2:
✓ D) Accountability

The Accountability principle states that AI creators should be responsible for how their systems operate and need to continuously monitor system performance and mitigate risks.

column1:
What technique does AI Privacy and Security use to replace personal details with random identifiers?

  • A) Aggregation
  • B) Anonymization
  • C) Pseudonymization
  • D) Tokenization
column2:
✓ C) Pseudonymization

Pseudonymization replaces personal details with random identifiers to protect identities. Aggregation groups data into summaries, removing specific individual details.

column1:
What are the four core prompt engineering principles for GitHub Copilot (the 4 S's)?

  • A) Simple, Structured, Short, Supported
  • B) Single, Specific, Short, Surround
  • C) Solid, Specific, Succinct, Scoped
  • D) Single, Structured, Succinct, Sourced
column2:
✓ B) Single, Specific, Short, Surround

The four core prompt engineering principles are: Single (focus on one task), Specific (explicit and detailed), Short (concise), and Surround (use descriptive filenames and keep related files open).

column1:
Which learning approach involves GitHub Copilot generating code with NO examples provided?

  • A) One-shot learning
  • B) Few-shot learning
  • C) Zero-shot learning
  • D) Transfer learning
column2:
✓ C) Zero-shot learning

Zero-shot learning is where GitHub Copilot generates code without any specific examples, relying solely on its foundational training. It's ideal for rapidly implementing common patterns.

column1:
Which learning approach provides a SINGLE example to help Copilot generate more context-aware responses?

  • A) Zero-shot learning
  • B) One-shot learning
  • C) Few-shot learning
  • D) Fine-tuning
column2:
✓ B) One-shot learning

One-shot learning provides a single example, aiding the model in generating more context-aware responses that follow your specific patterns and conventions.

column1:
What technique involves instructing Copilot to act as a domain expert (e.g., “Act as a cybersecurity expert”)?

  • A) Chain prompting
  • B) Context injection
  • C) Role prompting
  • D) Few-shot prompting
column2:
✓ C) Role prompting

Role prompting involves instructing GitHub Copilot to act as a specific type of expert (security expert, performance expert, testing specialist) to significantly improve the quality and relevance of generated code.

column1:
What does the Fill-in-the-Middle (FIM) technique do during Copilot's prompt processing?

  • A) Fills in missing variable names
  • B) Considers both preceding and following code context
  • C) Completes function signatures only
  • D) Generates middle sections of long functions
column2:
✓ B) Considers both preceding and following code context

The Fill-in-the-Middle (FIM) technique considers both the preceding and following code context, expanding the model's understanding to generate more accurate and relevant code suggestions.

column1:
For how many days does GitHub typically retain Copilot Chat prompts outside the code editor?

  • A) 7 days
  • B) 14 days
  • C) 28 days
  • D) 90 days
column2:
✓ C) 28 days

For Copilot Chat used outside the code editor, GitHub typically retains prompts, suggestions, and supporting context for 28 days.

column1:
What is the approximate context window size for GitHub Copilot Chat?

  • A) 1k tokens
  • B) 2k tokens
  • C) 4k tokens
  • D) 8k tokens
column2:
✓ C) 4k tokens

Copilot Chat operates with a context window of 4k tokens. The standard Copilot context window typically ranges from approximately 200–500 lines of code or up to a few thousand tokens.

column1:
What does LoRA stand for in the context of LLM fine-tuning?

  • A) Large Optimization and Retrieval Algorithm
  • B) Low-Rank Adaptation
  • C) Layered Output Ranking Analysis
  • D) Lightweight Output Regularization Approach
column2:
✓ B) Low-Rank Adaptation

LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation) fine-tuning adds smaller trainable parts to each layer of the pretrained model instead of retraining everything, saving time and resources while achieving great results.

column1:
What is a Copilot Space?

  • A) A dedicated IDE plugin for AI-assisted coding
  • B) A dedicated Copilot chat grounded in a curated set of context you choose
  • C) A cloud environment for running Copilot agents
  • D) A GitHub repository template for AI projects
column2:
✓ B) A dedicated Copilot chat grounded in a curated set of context you choose

A Copilot Space is a dedicated chat grounded in a curated set of context. You can feed it GitHub files, issues, pull requests, and free-text instructions focused on a specific topic.

column1:
Which statement BEST describes the security model of Copilot Spaces?

  • A) Spaces grant special read access to all organization repos
  • B) Spaces only surface content that viewers are already entitled to see
  • C) Spaces require admin approval before sharing with teammates
  • D) Spaces encrypt all context and restrict access to the Space owner
column2:
✓ B) Spaces only surface content that viewers are already entitled to see

A Space doesn't grant new access; it only surfaces content that viewers are already entitled to see based on their existing GitHub repo permissions.

column1:
What does the Copilot Coding Agent do?

  • A) Reviews pull requests automatically
  • B) Performs multi-step coding tasks autonomously based on your instructions
  • C) Monitors code quality metrics across the organization
  • D) Generates documentation from existing code comments
column2:
✓ B) Performs multi-step coding tasks autonomously based on your instructions

The Coding Agent acts as an autonomous AI assistant that can generate multiple related files, implement a feature set, and build scaffolding from a specification under your direction.

column1:
Which Copilot feature auto-generates a summary description of code changes to help reviewers?

  • A) Copilot Chat
  • B) Code Review Assistance
  • C) Code Review Summaries
  • D) Inline Suggestions
column2:
✓ C) Code Review Summaries

Code Review Summaries auto-generate a summary description of changes, helping reviewers understand intent, reducing the work in writing PR text, and improving clarity in collaboration.

column1:
What does the @terminal agent in VS Code allow you to do?

  • A) Open a new terminal window
  • B) Chat with GitHub Copilot to interact with the terminal
  • C) Execute code directly in the terminal without review
  • D) Monitor terminal output for errors
column2:
✓ B) Chat with GitHub Copilot to interact with the terminal

The @terminal agent is a context-specific agent that allows you to ask questions using the terminal as context and interact with GitHub Copilot about terminal tasks.

column1:
What is the purpose of the proxy filter in Copilot's prompt processing pipeline?

  • A) To compress prompts for faster transmission
  • B) To block attempts to hack the prompt or manipulate the system
  • C) To translate prompts into multiple programming languages
  • D) To cache frequently used prompts
column2:
✓ B) To block attempts to hack the prompt or manipulate the system

The proxy server filters traffic, blocking attempts to hack the prompt or manipulate the system into revealing details about how the model generates code suggestions.

column1:
Which Copilot plan FIRST introduces centralized management, policy controls, and security vulnerability filtering?

  • A) Copilot Pro+
  • B) Copilot Business
  • C) Copilot Enterprise
  • D) Copilot Pro
column2:
✓ B) Copilot Business

GitHub Copilot Business is the first plan to include centralized management, policy controls, security vulnerability filtering, code referencing and public code filtering, and IP indemnity for organizations.

column1:
What is the Copilot Plan Agent's primary purpose?

  • A) To generate code immediately based on your description
  • B) To create a structured implementation plan before coding starts
  • C) To review and approve pull requests
  • D) To run automated test suites
column2:
✓ B) To create a structured implementation plan before coding starts

The Copilot Plan Agent uses read-only research to understand requirements, asks clarifying questions, and creates a structured plan. It does not edit files until you approve the plan and hand off to Agent Mode.

column1:
When the optional public code filter is enabled, suggestions over ~how many characters that resemble public code are blocked?

  • A) 50 characters
  • B) 100 characters
  • C) 150 characters
  • D) 200 characters
column2:
✓ C) 150 characters

When enabled, the filter prevents Copilot from returning suggestions over ~150 characters if they closely resemble existing public code on GitHub, preventing coincidental matches from being suggested as original content.

column1:
Which best practice helps manage long Copilot Chat conversation histories efficiently?

  • A) Always start a new chat for every question
  • B) Summarize context when conversations become lengthy
  • C) Limit prompts to 10 words or fewer
  • D) Only use slash commands to avoid context buildup
column2:
✓ B) Summarize context when conversations become lengthy

To manage chat history efficiently, summarize context when conversations grow long (e.g., "Based on our previous discussion about X, now add Y"), reset for new features, and use concise references to previous work.
